title: iberis ciliata subsp. lagascana
intro: Iberis ciliata subsp. lagascana, also known as Candytuft or Rock Candy, is a flowering plant species in the mustard family (Brassicaceae), native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Iberis ciliata subsp. lagascana include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm coughs, and reduce fever, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
